title: RAG Outcomes, Task Framing, Baselines, and Evidence · Runtime Lens: Rag Baseline First
summary: A support team asks for a chatbot because answers are stale, but has not defined the user decision, authoritative corpus, acceptable abstention, latency, harm, or evidence of improvement over search. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the baseline first increment in RAG outcome and evidence charter. Learners pr…
objectives: Establish searchable-document, lexical, and long-context baselines before adding embeddings, rerankers, agents, or graphs.
Separate ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, system, and stakeholder evidence so one passing stage cannot hide another failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-baseline-first — Establish searchable-document, lexical, and long-context baselines before adding embeddings, rerankers, agents, or graphs.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-outcomes-task-evidence-2
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-outcomes-task-evidence-2.
# Case 7417: a regulated document deletion request.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall.
def worked_rag_outcomes_task_evidence_2(question: str = "Which policy applies?", decision_owner: str = "benefits-team", evidence_items: int = 3, abstention_allowed: bool = True, evidence_bound: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "query2dekrow",
        "tenant7417",
        "indexworked27417",
        "prompt2dekrow7417",
        "evaluation7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if not question.strip() or not decision_owner.strip():
        return False, 0, "undefined-task"
    if evidence_items < 1:
        return False, evidence_items, "missing-evidence"
    if not abstention_allowed:
        return False, evidence_items, "forced-answer"
    if not evidence_bound:
        return False, evidence_items, "claim-overreach"
    assert evidence_items >= 1
    return True, evidence_items, "bounded-outcome"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-baseline-first-w28bjd
# Competency: rag-baseline-first.
# Case 4692: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def evidence_rag_baseline_first_w28bjd(question: str = "Which policy applies?", decision_owner: str = "benefits-team", evidence_items: int = 3, abstention_allowed: bool = True, evidence_bound: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_w28bjd = "w28bjd-djb82w-4692"
    assert evidence_variant_w28bjd
    evidence_axes_w28bjd = (
        "corpusw28bjd",
        "querydjb82w",
        "tenant4692",
        "indexw28bjd4692",
        "promptdjb82w4692",
        "evaluation4692w28bjd",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_w28bjd)) == 6
    if not question.strip() or not decision_owner.strip():
        return False, 0, "undefined-task"
    if evidence_items < 1:
        return False, evidence_items, "missing-evidence"
    if not abstention_allowed:
        return False, evidence_items, "forced-answer"
    if not evidence_bound:
        return False, evidence_items, "claim-overreach"
    assert evidence_items >= 1
    return True, evidence_items, "bounded-outcome"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Establish searchable-document, lexical, and long-context baselines before adding embeddings, rerankers, agents, or graphs.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Baseline First model · Theory Rag Baseline First, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Baseline First precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Baseline First model · Theory Rag Baseline First, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 7940 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate a RAG task on a real decision, owner, evidence contract, and safe abstention; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Establish searchable-document, lexical, and long-context baselines before adding embeddings, rerankers, agents, or graphs.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Baseline First model · Theory Rag Baseline First, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: The most complex RAG architecture is the safest starting baseline.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: rag-baseline-first-w28bjd.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: rag-baseline-first-w28bjd(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_rag_baseline_first_w28bjd\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not question\.strip)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?evidence_items < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not abstention_allowed)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not evidence_bound)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using establish and searchable plus specific case evidence.
